<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: summarize by coderabbit.ai -->
<!-- walkthrough_start -->

## Walkthrough

This change introduces a comprehensive "Tool Output Transformers" framework to preprocess and summarize large tool outputs before passing them to the main LLM, primarily to manage context window usage. It implements configuration options at global, toolset, and tool levels, provides a registry for transformers, and adds a built-in LLM summarization transformer. Extensive documentation, CLI/config integration, YAML toolset updates, and a full suite of unit and integration tests are included.

## Changes

| File(s)                                                                 | Change Summary |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------|
| README.md, docs/transformers.md                                         | Added detailed documentation on Tool Output Transformers, configuration, usage, and troubleshooting. |
| config.example.yaml, holmes/config.py, holmes/main.py                   | Added global config and CLI options for fast model summarization and summarization threshold; integrated into config loading and environment variable support. |
| holmes/core/transformers/* (base.py, llm_summarize.py, registry.py, validation.py, __init__.py) | Implemented transformer base class, registry, validation utilities, and built-in LLM summarization transformer. |
| holmes/core/tools.py, holmes/core/toolset_manager.py, holmes/utils/config_utils.py | Integrated transformer configs into tool/toolset invocation, inheritance, and merging; added config merging utility. |
| holmes/plugins/toolsets/aks-node-health.yaml, holmes/plugins/toolsets/aks.yaml, holmes/plugins/toolsets/kubernetes.yaml, holmes/plugins/toolsets/kubernetes_logs.yaml | Updated YAML toolsets to include transformer configurations for summarizing large outputs with custom prompts and thresholds. |
| tests/config_class/test_config_transformers.py, tests/core/test_config_transformers.py | Added tests for config fields, environment/CLI loading, inheritance, and auto-generation of transformer configs. |
| tests/core/test_tool_transformers.py, tests/core/test_toolset_manager.py, tests/core/test_transformer_backwards_compatibility.py | Added tests for transformer config propagation, validation, execution, chaining, error handling, and backward compatibility. |
| tests/core/transformers/* (test_llm_summarize.py, test_transformers.py, test_validation.py, __init__.py) | Added unit and integration tests for transformer base, registry, validation, and LLM summarization transformer. |
| tests/integration/test_config_merging_integration.py, tests/integration/test_kubernetes_transformer_execution.py, tests/integration/test_tool_execution_pipeline.py | Added integration tests for config merging, transformer execution in tool pipelines, error handling, and performance logging. |
| tests/plugins/toolsets/test_aks_transformers.py, tests/plugins/toolsets/test_kubernetes_transformers.py | Added tests for YAML toolset transformer config loading, prompt correctness, thresholds, and inheritance. |
| tests/utils/test_config_utils.py                                        | Added tests for config merging utility, covering edge cases and precedence logic. |

## Sequence Diagram(s)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant CLI/Config
    participant Holmes Core
    participant ToolsetManager
    participant Tool
    participant TransformerRegistry
    participant Transformer (LLMSummarize)
    participant LLM (Fast Model)
    participant Main LLM

    User->>CLI/Config: Specify fast_model, summarize_threshold, or YAML toolset configs
    CLI/Config->>Holmes Core: Load configuration (global/toolset/tool-level)
    Holmes Core->>ToolsetManager: Initialize with global transformer configs
    ToolsetManager->>Tool: Merge/inherit transformer configs
    User->>Tool: Invoke tool
    Tool->>Tool: Run command, produce output
    Tool->>TransformerRegistry: For each transformer config, instantiate transformer
    Tool->>Transformer (LLMSummarize): Check should_apply (threshold, fast_model)
    alt should_apply == true
        Transformer (LLMSummarize)->>LLM (Fast Model): Summarize output with prompt
        LLM (Fast Model)->>Transformer (LLMSummarize): Return summary
        Transformer (LLMSummarize)->>Tool: Return summarized output
    else should_apply == false or error
        Transformer (LLMSummarize)->>Tool: Return original output
    end
    Tool->>Main LLM: Send (possibly summarized) output for further analysis
```

## Estimated code review effort

🎯 5 (Critical) | ⏱️ ~180–240 minutes

## Suggested labels

`enhancement`

## Suggested reviewers

- aantn  
- arikalon1

<details>
<summary>🪧 Tips</summary>

### Chat

There are 3 ways to chat with [CodeRabbit](https://coderabbit.ai?utm_source=oss&utm_medium=github&utm_campaign=robusta-dev/holmesgpt&utm_content=695):

- Review comments: Directly reply to a review comment made by CodeRabbit. Example:
  - `I pushed a fix in commit <commit_id>, please review it.`
  - `Explain this complex logic.`
  - `Open a follow-up GitHub issue for this discussion.`
- Files and specific lines of code (under the "Files changed" tab): Tag `@coderabbitai` in a new review comment at the desired location with your query. Examples:
  - `@coderabbitai explain this code block.`
  -	`@coderabbitai modularize this function.`
- PR comments: Tag `@coderabbitai` in a new PR comment to ask questions about the PR branch. For the best results, please provide a very specific query, as very limited context is provided in this mode. Examples:
  - `@coderabbitai gather interesting stats about this repository and render them as a table. Additionally, render a pie chart showing the language distribution in the codebase.`
  - `@coderabbitai read src/utils.ts and explain its main purpose.`
  - `@coderabbitai read the files in the src/scheduler package and generate a class diagram using mermaid and a README in the markdown format.`
  - `@coderabbitai help me debug CodeRabbit configuration file.`

### Support

Need help? Create a ticket on our [support page](https://www.coderabbit.ai/contact-us/support) for assistance with any issues or questions.

Note: Be mindful of the bot's finite context window. It's strongly recommended to break down tasks such as reading entire modules into smaller chunks. For a focused discussion, use review comments to chat about specific files and their changes, instead of using the PR comments.

### CodeRabbit Commands (Invoked using PR comments)

- `@coderabbitai pause` to pause the reviews on a PR.
- `@coderabbitai resume` to resume the paused reviews.
- `@coderabbitai review` to trigger an incremental review. This is useful when automatic reviews are disabled for the repository.
- `@coderabbitai full review` to do a full review from scratch and review all the files again.
- `@coderabbitai summary` to regenerate the summary of the PR.
- `@coderabbitai generate docstrings` to [generate docstrings](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/finishing-touches/docstrings) for this PR.
- `@coderabbitai generate sequence diagram` to generate a sequence diagram of the changes in this PR.
- `@coderabbitai generate unit tests` to generate unit tests for this PR.
- `@coderabbitai resolve` resolve all the CodeRabbit review comments.
- `@coderabbitai configuration` to show the current CodeRabbit configuration for the repository.
- `@coderabbitai help` to get help.

### Other keywords and placeholders

- Add `@coderabbitai ignore` anywhere in the PR description to prevent this PR from being reviewed.
- Add `@coderabbitai summary` to generate the high-level summary at a specific location in the PR description.
- Add `@coderabbitai` anywhere in the PR title to generate the title automatically.

### CodeRabbit Configuration File (`.coderabbit.yaml`)

- You can programmatically configure CodeRabbit by adding a `.coderabbit.yaml` file to the root of your repository.
- Please see the [configuration documentation](https://docs.coderabbit.ai/guides/configure-coderabbit) for more information.
- If your editor has YAML language server enabled, you can add the path at the top of this file to enable auto-completion and validation: `# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://coderabbit.ai/integrations/schema.v2.json`

### Documentation and Community

- Visit our [Documentation](https://docs.coderabbit.ai) for detailed information on how to use CodeRabbit.
- Join our [Discord Community](http://discord.gg/coderabbit) to get help, request features, and share feedback.
- Follow us on [X/Twitter](https://twitter.com/coderabbitai) for updates and announcements.

</details>

<!-- tips_end -->
